open_questions: EAS project linking: Does `eas init --id <projectId>` support non-interactive use? If not, writing expo.extra.eas.projectId directly to app.json is the fallback. Confirm preferred approach before implementing.

tasks.title: Add ExpoReadinessResponse type to shared package
tasks.description: Add ExpoReadinessResponse interface to packages/shared/src/types/deploy.ts with expoInstalled: boolean, expoConfigured: boolean, authOk: boolean, easProjectLinked: boolean, missing: string[], prompt?: string. Export from shared package. Verify with: npm run build in packages/shared

tasks.title: Add isEasProjectLinked utility for EAS project detection
tasks.description: Create a new utility function that checks if an Expo project is linked to EAS. Check app.json/app.config.js for expo.extra.eas.projectId first; if not found, optionally run `npx eas-cli project:info` (or equivalent) to detect linked project. Return boolean. Handle errors gracefully (e.g. EAS CLI not installed, network failure) by returning false. File: packages/backend/src/utils/expo-eas-linked.ts. Add unit tests in packages/backend/src/utils/__tests__/expo-eas-linked.test.ts mocking exec. Verify with: npm test -- expo-eas-linked

tasks.title: Add GET /projects/:projectId/deliver/expo-readiness endpoint
tasks.description: Add GET /projects/:projectId/deliver/expo-readiness route in packages/backend/src/routes/deliver.ts (before /:deployId so path is not captured). Returns 200 with { data: { expoInstalled: boolean, expoConfigured: boolean, authOk: boolean, easProjectLinked: boolean, missing: string[], prompt?: string } }. Use isExpoInstalled (expo-install.ts), getExpoConfigStatus (expo-config.ts), checkExpoAuth (expo-auth-check.ts), and isEasProjectLinked (new). Build missing array from false checks; include prompt from checkExpoAuth when authOk is false. Return 400 when deployment.mode !== 'expo'; 404 when project not found; 500 on invalid repo path. Verify with: npm test -- deploy-route
dependsOn: Add ExpoReadinessResponse type to shared package
Add isEasProjectLinked utility for EAS project detection

tasks.title: Add easProjectId to DeploymentConfig and extend PUT deliver/settings
tasks.description: Add optional easProjectId?: string to DeploymentConfig in packages/shared/src/types/settings.ts when mode is expo. Update migrateDeploymentConfig to accept and persist easProjectId. Ensure PUT /projects/:projectId/deliver/settings accepts easProjectId in the deployment body. The existing route merges deployment into settings; no backend route change needed beyond ensuring the type allows it. Add migration for legacy projects. Verify with: npm test -- settings.test

tasks.title: Implement EAS project linking before first deploy
tasks.description: When easProjectId (or expoConfig.projectId) is set in deployment settings, before running eas deploy in runExpoDeployAsync: (1) Check if app.json already has expo.extra.eas.projectId; (2) If not, write expo.extra.eas.projectId into app.json, or run `npx eas-cli init --id <id>` non-interactively if supported. Prefer writing app.json directly for reliability. Add to runExpoDeployAsync in packages/backend/src/routes/deliver.ts after ensureExpoConfig and before ensureEasConfig. On failure, emit error to log and complete deploy with failure. Verify with: npm test -- deliver
dependsOn: Add easProjectId to DeploymentConfig and extend PUT deliver/settings

tasks.title: Add expo-readiness API client and query hook
tasks.description: Add api.deliver.expoReadiness(projectId) in packages/frontend/src/api/client.ts returning the readiness response. Add queryKeys.deliver.expoReadiness(projectId) in packages/frontend/src/api/queryKeys.ts. Add useExpoReadiness(projectId) hook in packages/frontend/src/api/hooks/deliver.ts that fetches when projectId is set and deployment mode is expo. Export ExpoReadinessResponse type from shared or define in frontend. Verify with: npm test -- client.test
dependsOn: Add ExpoReadinessResponse type to shared package

tasks.title: Add Expo readiness banner to Deliver phase when auth missing
tasks.description: In DeliverPhase.tsx, when mode is expo: fetch expo-readiness on mount and when deploy mode is Expo. When readiness indicates !authOk, show a prominent banner above deploy buttons: 'Expo deployment requires an access token. Add it in Settings → Expo API Token.' with 'Open Settings' button that calls onOpenGlobalSettings (or onOpenSettings). Reuse existing expoDeployError handling pattern. Banner should appear before user clicks Deploy. Deploy buttons remain enabled; auth failure still returns 400 with existing prompt. Verify with: npm test -- DeliverPhase
dependsOn: Add expo-readiness API client and query hook

tasks.title: Add optional EAS project ID field to Project Settings deployment tab
tasks.description: In ProjectSettingsModal.tsx, when deployment.mode === 'expo', add an optional 'EAS Project ID' input field in the Expo section (below or alongside channel). Label: 'EAS Project ID'. Helper text: 'Link to an existing Expo project. Leave blank to create one on first deploy.' Store in deployment.expoConfig.projectId (or easProjectId if added to type). Ensure updateDeployment persists it. Verify with: npm test -- ProjectSettingsModal
dependsOn: Add easProjectId to DeploymentConfig and extend PUT deliver/settings

tasks.title: Add Setup status / Ready to deploy indicator for Expo in Deliver phase
tasks.description: When expo-readiness returns all checks true (expoInstalled, expoConfigured, authOk, easProjectLinked), show a brief 'Ready to deploy' or green check indicator in the Deliver phase toolbar or above deploy buttons. When any check is false, the existing auth banner (from prior task) or a compact 'Setup required' state is shown. Optional: when expoInstalled or expoConfigured is false, show 'Setting up Expo…' during first deploy (live log already shows this). No blocking modal. Verify with: npm test -- DeliverPhase
dependsOn: Add Expo readiness banner to Deliver phase when auth missing

tasks.title: Unit tests for expo-readiness route
tasks.description: Add unit tests in packages/backend/src/__tests__/ for the expo-readiness endpoint. Mock isExpoInstalled, getExpoConfigStatus, checkExpoAuth, isEasProjectLinked. Test: (1) 200 with all true returns correct shape; (2) 200 with authOk false includes missing and prompt; (3) 400 when mode is not expo; (4) 404 when project not found; (5) missing array correctly populated from false checks. Verify with: npm test -- deploy-route
